threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think it's normal to complain about the bus being late. Locals just shake their heads and carry on, knowing it's part of the charm.
Caught the N37 last night, and the driver was locking eyes with a guy serenading the whole bus. It was oddly beautiful, but I bet the late-night crowd didn’t appreciate it.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw my neighbor's pet goat munching on garden weeds while their toddler ran around in a superhero cape. Classic Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you stumble upon a kiosk doubling as an art gallery at 2 AM, selling currywurst and local art side by side.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by their reaction to the BART delays—judging when to sprint for the bus instead of wasting time at the Civic Center.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy serenading the bus stop at Turk and Jones, guitar in hand, while the Muni was late as usual. Only in SF do delays become concerts.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all square terraces are for sipping sangria, but locals actually use them for late-night guitar sessions and neighborhood gossip.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by how they still squint at the metro map, holding it upside down, while locals glide past effortlessly.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still wait for the Tube to be on time—locals know to check the bus app first, even when strikes are off.
Caught a night bus from Brixton to Clapham last weekend. A fella tried to sell me a half-eaten kebab. Said it’s 'authentic late-night fare.'

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: Heard a kid on the street asking for 'taku' instead of taxi. That’s when I knew Neukölln had truly shaped my language.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you sip a beer on the banks of the Spree while a guy in a tutu juggles flaming torches. It’s the little moments that define Berlin.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know they're locals when they dodge the Muni delays like a game of Frogger, never breaking stride even as the bus stops for a dog on the tracks.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Just saw a guy with a saxophone serenading the morning commute at Civic Center. Muni delays? Sure. But the music made it feel alive.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think 'pintxos' are just fancy tapas, but in Gràcia, they’re the 2am snack of choice after a long night out, often from a hole-in-the-wall.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell by the way they hesitate at the Glòries roundabout, looking around like a lost tourist despite living here for weeks.
